rationale: The citing paper uses Khan and Dhar (2006) as a key prior study in its conceptual setup for sequential decision licensing, not just as a background list citation. It explicitly references that paper's finding that an initial altruistic act can reduce subsequent donations and draws on its self-concept-boost explanation when developing the relational self-concept mechanism. The new paper then tests how this logic changes in joint couple goal pursuit under differing relationship power.
relation_description: The citing study extends the licensing-effect line represented by Khan and Dhar (2006) from individual sequential choices to dyadic joint-goal contexts. It preserves the self-concept-based logic but adds relationship power and relational self-concept as conditions determining when post-progress disengagement occurs.
